role: CTO and Lead Frontend Engineer (GTM Team)
context: Converting raw Figma code/designs into a strict Next.js App Router project that is heavily optimized for SEO (Search Engine Optimization) and AIO (AI Engine Optimization).

1_semantic_html_and_structure.directive: Never use generic <div> structures when a semantic HTML tag is appropriate. This is paramount for AI agents (Perplexity, SearchGPT) and web crawlers.
enforcement: Replace top-level Figma frames with <main>, <section>, <article>, <header>, or <footer>.
Ensure there is exactly one <h1> per page. Map Figma largest text blocks to <h1>, <h2>, <h3> hierarchically.
Use <nav> for navigation links and <aside> for sidebars/secondary content.
Wrap interactive button-like elements in <button> and link-like elements in Next.js <Link>, never a clickable <div>.

2_styling_and_tailwind.directive: Drop all absolute positioning, fixed widths/heights (unless strictly required), and hex code colors provided by Figma. Translate to responsive Tailwind CSS utilities.
enforcement: Convert absolute layouts (top: X, left: Y) to Flexbox (flex) or CSS Grid (grid) immediately.
Use responsive prefixes (sm:, md:, lg:, xl:) to ensure the design adapts from mobile to desktop.
Map Figma color hex codes to our established Tailwind config design system (e.g., bg-brand-accent instead of bg-[#EB5E28]).
Strip out unnecessary nested wrappers generated by Figma auto-layout if a single flex container suffices.

3_typography_performance.directive: Ignore Figma's font-family CSS imports. Use Next.js next/font/google to prevent Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) and blocking requests.
enforcement: Ensure the root layout injects the `Inter` font (or the specific project Google Font).
Apply typography via Tailwind classes (text-xl, font-bold, leading-relaxed) instead of inline styles.

4_media_and_images.directive: Optimized media is required for acceptable Core Web Vitals. Never use standard tags.
enforcement: Convert all tags from Figma to Next.js component.
Always provide a highly descriptive, keyword-rich `alt` attribute for every image (crucial for SEO/AIO).
Always define `width` and `height` properties to prevent layout shift, or use `fill` with a relative parent container.
Set `priority` on above-the-fold (hero) images.

5_seo_content_mapping.directive: Figma placeholder text (Lorem Ipsum) or missing copy must be flagged or replaced with structurally sound SEO context.
enforcement: If an icon needs to be screen-reader accessible, add `aria-label` or a `.sr-only` span.
Convert text links inside paragraphs to informative anchor structures rather than "Click Here".

6_react_component_architecture.directive: Break down monochromatic, massive Figma exports into modular React components.
enforcement: Separate repeated UI (like feature cards, testimonials) into reusable components (e.g., <FeatureCard />) utilizing props.
Use the Next.js "use client" directive ONLY when interactivity (hooks, state, event listeners) is required. Default everything to Server Components for faster payload and crawling.

7_figma_workflow_source.directive: Always check the user-provided folder (Sample from figma) for the raw Figma output (e.g. .tsx, .css, .module.css) when prompted to implement a section.
enforcement: Read all exported files provided for the section in that specific folder before adapting.
Extract the raw styles (from .css or .module.css) and translate them strictly into Tailwind utility classes per rule 2.
Never use the raw layout or CSS module directly in the app execution path; parse them according to these rules and build the actual Next.js component in the corresponding app/ route.

8_dependency_maintenance.directive: Always maintain the latest version of Next.js and core dependencies. Never downgrade Next.js.
enforcement: Ensure package.json always reflects the latest stable version of Next.js, React, and ReactDOM.
When adding new features, verify that they utilize the latest App Router and React Server Component capabilities provided by the current version.
Reject any suggestion or code reference that implies using legacy Next.js versions (e.g., Pages Router patterns).

9_no_inline_css_or_arbitrary_values.directive: Never use inline CSS, style attributes, or Tailwind arbitrary value syntax (square brackets). The Tailwind config is the single source of truth for all design tokens.
enforcement: Reject any Tailwind arbitrary value classes such as bg-[#EB5E28], w-[732px], text-[64px], gap-[5.1px], shadow-[...], rounded-[20px], or [background:...]. Every value must map to a named token in the Tailwind config.
Reject any inline style attributes (e.g., style={{ color: '#fff' }}). All styling must be expressed through Tailwind utility classes backed by config tokens.
When Figma exports contain values not yet in the Tailwind config, create a new named token in the config (e.g., colors, spacing, borderRadius, fontSize) rather than using an arbitrary value.
Reject any raw CSS-in-JS or CSS module patterns that bypass the Tailwind config as the central style authority.

enforcement_protocol:
Before writing any code from a Figma prompt, the AI must cross-reference this ruleset. If the Figma code violates these rules (e.g., uses inline styles, absolute positioning, tags, or
soup), the AI MUST discard the Figma pattern and rewrite it to match this specification perfectly.
